_comment: The document lists HBP 1.0 codes S500074, S100015 and HBP 2.0 code SB065A. The primary procedure code for this object is SB065A.
min_doctor_qualification: Diploma in Orthopedics with 10 years of experience
clinical_indications: Patients who do not respond to conservative treatment for bursitis
Symptoms of bursitis persist after conservative treatment

preauth.label: Clinical notes detailing findings confirming the diagnosis
Clinical photograph of the affected part
X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) - affected part justifying the indication

ppd_preauth.q: Did X-ray report confirm the diagnosis of bursitis?

common_queries: X-ray report did not confirm the diagnosis of bursitis.
Clinical notes do not contain findings confirming the diagnosis.
Clinical photograph of the affected part is missing or unclear at pre-authorization.
X-ray of the affected part is not labelled with patient ID, date, or side (Left/Right), or does not justify the indication.
Conservative treatment failure prior to considering surgical intervention is not documented.
Post-procedure clinical photograph or detailed operative notes are missing at claim submission.

clinical_key_pointers: Olecranon and prepatellar bursitis are common conditions.
They are caused by repetitive minor trauma, acute trauma, and inflammatory diseases such as gout and rheumatoid arthritis.
The patients usually complain about pain and swelling.
Most of the aseptic bursitis cases responded to conservative treatment such as ice, rest, anti-inflammatory drugs, aspiration, and local corticosteroid and lidocaine injections.
surgical_options: Total excision through an open or endoscopic total excision
additional_information.referral_criteria: Refer for orthopedic consultation to consider surgical intervention when symptoms do not resolve after conservative treatment.
